previous_statement: N1.1 (BASE, pure algebra). Writing S_F := 2F - I_A in algebra A and S_Phi := 2Phi - I_B in algebra B, the intertwiner relation lifts to the shifted elements: S_F j = j S_Phi (as bounded linear maps B -> A). DERIVATION: S_F j = (2F - I_A) j = 2 (F j) - I_A j. By GT-hyp (H5) F j = j Phi; and I_A j = j (the identity on the A-side composed with j is j itself), while j I_B = j and by GT-hyp (H4) j(I_B)=I_A so j applied with the B-unit is consistent; precisely I_A j = j = j I_B as maps B->A. Hence S_F j = 2 (j Phi) - j = j (2Phi) - j = j (2Phi - I_B) = j S_Phi, using linearity of j (GT-hyp H3: j linear) to pull the scalar 2 and the unit I_B inside, and j I_B = j. Therefore S_F j = j S_Phi. Uses GT-hyp.
new_statement: N1.1 (BASE, pure algebra). Writing S_F := 2F - I_A in algebra A and S_Phi := 2Phi - I_B in algebra B, the intertwiner relation lifts to the shifted elements: S_F j = j S_Phi (as bounded linear maps B -> A). DERIVATION: S_F j = (2F - I_A) j = 2 (F j) - I_A j (linearity of composition). By GT-hyp (H5) F j = j Phi. For the unit terms, only the MULTIPLICATIVE-UNIT property is used (NOT unitality j(I_B)=I_A): I_A j = j because I_A is the unit of A acting by left multiplication, so for all y in B, (I_A j)(y) = I_A . j(y) = j(y); and j I_B = j because I_B is the unit of B, so j I_B (y) = j(I_B . y) = j(y). Hence I_A j = j = j I_B as maps B -> A, using ONLY that I_A, I_B act as the identity operator (H1: A, B unital Banach algebras), independent of any value of j(I_B). Therefore S_F j = 2 (j Phi) - j = j (2Phi) - j = j (2Phi - I_B) = j S_Phi, using linearity of j (GT-hyp H3) to pull the scalar 2 and the unit I_B inside, and j I_B = j. Therefore S_F j = j S_Phi. [Remark: this is the H4-free form; unitality j(I_B)=I_A is NOT load-bearing here or anywhere in nodes 1.1-1.6.] Uses GT-hyp.
